[Source]
Title: Founder Story: Harrison Chase of LangChain - Frederick AI
URL: https://www.frederick.ai/blog/harrison-chase-langchain
Content: Harrison Chase, the visionary co-founder of LangChain, has redefined how developers harness Language Learning Models (LLMs) through a revolutionary open-source framework. His work at LangChain not only empowers developers to build context-aware applications but also sets new standards for collaboration in the AI ecosystem. The idea for LangChain took root in 2022 when Chase recognized a recurring challenge: developers were struggling to build reliable LLM applications due to fragmented tools and insufficient abstractions. This tool cemented LangChain's reputation as not only a framework but also a robust ecosystem for LLM development. * **Community-Driven Development**: By keeping LangChain open-source, Chase encouraged contributions from global developers, leading to faster iterations and richer feature sets. Beyond LangChain, Chase remains passionate about democratizing AI, ensuring that innovative tools are accessible to developers of all skill levels. Through LangChain, he has not only simplified the mechanics of LLM development but also fostered a culture of collaboration that continues to propel the AI community forward.

[Source]
Title: People to Watch 2024 - Harrison Chase - BigDATAwire - HPCwire
URL: https://www.hpcwire.com/bigdatawire/people-to-watch-2024-harrison-chase/
Content: Harrison Chase is the co-founder and CEO of LangChain, developer of the eponymous tool for chaining together interoperable components for GenAI applications. Prior to founding LangChain in late 2022, Chase was a machine learning engineer at Kensho Technologies, an MLOps company focused on testing and validation of machine learning models, and led the entity linking team at Kensho, a fintech startup. Under Chase’s guidance, the San Francisco-based company has developed the LangChain product into an open source powerhouse, with more than 15 million monthly downloads, more than 75,000 GitHub stars, and more than 3,000 contributors for the project. [Source]
Title: Harrison Chase - The AI Conference
URL: https://aiconference.com/speakers/harrison-chase/
Content: ## Harrison Chase. ###### Co-Founder and CEO Langchain. Harrison Chase Context Aware Reasoning Applications v2. ## Presentation Title:. ## Building reliable LLM applications with LangChain and LangSmith. ## Presentation Summary:. It’s become easier and easier to build a prototype for an LLM application – with LangChain you can do it in ~5 lines of code. But going from prototype to production is much more difficult, which is where LangSmith comes in. This talk will cover the emerging journey an aspiring AI engineer embarks on, from prototyping to debugging to testing to evaluating to monitoring and everything in between! #### about | Harrison Chase. Harrison Chase is the co-founder and CEO of LangChain, a company formed around the open-source Python/Typescript packages that aim to make it easy to develop Language Model applications. Prior to starting LangChain, he led the ML team at Robust Intelligence (an MLOps company focused on testing and validation of machine learning models), led the entity linking team at Kensho (a fintech startup), and studied stats and CS at Harvard.

[Source]
Title: Harrison Chase, LangChain CEO - Interview - YouTube
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7D8bw_4hTdo
Content: Harrison Chase, LangChain CEO - Interview MindsDB 3590 subscribers 15 likes 1233 views 19 Apr 2024 Join us for an insightful interview with Harrison Chase, the CEO and co-founder of Langchain, as he provides a comprehensive overview of Langchain's innovative developer framework. Discover how Langchain simplifies the creation of complex language model applications, connecting them seamlessly with external data sources and enabling agentic reasoning. From the origins of Langchain to its unexpected applications, Harrison shares valuable insights into the future of AI development and the challenges of navigating AI regulation. Don't miss this engaging discussion on the intersection of technology, innovation, and responsible AI usage. This interview was recorded on June 1st 2023, at the MindsDB Con event in San Francisco

[Source]
Title: Harrison Chase | TEDAI San Francisco - TED Talks
URL: https://tedai-sanfrancisco.ted.com/speakers/harrison-chase/
Content: ## TEDAI 2025 - Home Page - AI Conference at San Francisco## TEDAI Talks - Featured Speakers and Presentations## TEDAI Panels - Expert Discussions and Industry Insights## TEDAI Hackathon - Innovation Competition. # Harrison Chase. Co-Founder and CEO, LangChain. ## The Magical AI Assistants of the Future — and the Engineering Behind them. Harrison Chase, a Harvard graduate in statistics and computer science, co-founded LangChain to streamline the development of Language Model applications with open-source Python/Typescript packages. Chase's experience includes heading the Machine Learning team at Robust Intelligence, focusing on the testing and validation of machine learning models, and leading the entity linking team at Kensho, a fintech startup. © 2025 by TEDAI San Francisco. © 2025 by TEDAI San Francisco. © 2025 by TEDAI San Francisco.

Title: LangChain's Harrison Chase on Building the Orchestration Layer for ...
URL: https://sequoiacap.com/podcast/training-data-harrison-chase/
Content: But I do think they’ll be more along the lines of an agent doing something and maybe checking in with you, as opposed to a copilot that’s constantly kind of like in the loop, I just think it’s, I just think it’s more powerful and gives you more leverage if the more that they’re doing, which, which is very paradoxical as well, because it comes, the more the more you let it do things by itself, there’s more risk that it’s messing up or going off the rails. And so I think in practice, what we’re seeing with these agents is they’re much more kind of like custom cognitive architectures is kind of like what we call them, where there’s a certain way of doing things that you generally want an agent to do. **Harrison Chase:** Yeah, I think there’s, maybe it’s worth talking a little bit about why kind of like the AutoGPT, things didn’t didn’t work. There’s, there’s one other kind of UX thing that I think is interesting to think about, which is basically just like, how do these agents learn from these interactions, right?

The founder of LangChain is **Harrison Chase**. He is recognized as the co-founder and CEO of LangChain, an open-source framework designed to simplify the development of applications using large language models (LLMs). Chase established LangChain in late 2022, aiming to provide developers with tools to build context-aware and interoperable GenAI applications. His work has positioned LangChain as a pivotal ecosystem in the AI development community.
